summary: G2G iteration 3 testing — wrote new pytest /app/backend/tests/test_iteration3.py with 18 tests covering blocks removal, trust-score rules, reports, admin users (suspend/unsuspend), wallet topup/transactions, likes, categories counts, full refund flow (accept/reject/escalate/messages/evidence), admin refunds (force_refund/force_reject/resolve), and admin analytics. ALL 18 BACKEND TESTS PASS (18/18). Frontend Playwright smoke verified admin auto-redirect to /admin and route guards (admin → /dashboard, /buy → /admin), aria sidebar /liked link, /liked page renders saved items, Buy page category counts, Wallet page renders with Escrow/Refund sections + Top up button opens dialog, /match shows Leaflet map + Map/Grid toggle, ProductDetail no Block button.

minor.issue: Response only returns {users, listings, payments, revenue, pendingKyc}. The review spec required openReports, openRefunds, verifiedUsers AND users should EXCLUDE admin. Current users count includes admin (21 users instead of 20).

New verified user's trustScore is 10 (emailVerified=true bumps +10) not strictly 0. Spec says 'trust score starts at 0 for new users'. Either lock min until KYC, or this minor mismatch with PRD wording.

ui_bugs.issue: Report button not found on /product/{id} page via role=button name=Report. Block button correctly absent (good). Report action may be hidden in an overflow menu OR removed entirely. Verify Report is still surfaced to end users per spec.
Dialog opens after clicking 'Top up wallet' but content does not contain the literal word 'Amount' — label may be styled differently or use placeholder text. Not blocking, just note.

action_items: Extend /api/admin/analytics to include openReports, openRefunds, verifiedUsers and exclude admins from users count (review spec explicitly required).
Verify that the Report action on ProductDetail is still accessible (button or menu item) — automated test could not locate it.
Consider clamping new-user trustScore to 0 until KYC approved, or update PRD wording (currently 10 due to email verification).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py now 1418 lines — overdue to split into modules (auth, listings, refunds, wallet, admin, chat). Maintainability risk grows each iteration.
/api/admin/analytics: missing required fields per spec (openReports/openRefunds/verifiedUsers) and includes admin in user count.
/api/refunds/{id}/evidence accepts raw `body: dict` instead of a Pydantic model — same anti-pattern previously flagged for listings PATCH.
Token = user_id (plain) and passwords stored plaintext — acceptable per university-demo decision but flag for any production hand-off.
Likes endpoint /api/likes/{listing_id} POST does upsert silently — no 404 if listing_id doesn't exist. Could leak orphan likes.

success_rate.frontend: ~90% — admin guard, liked page, smart-match leaflet, wallet, buy category counts, product detail (no block) all verified visually

test_credentials: Read from /app/memory/test_credentials.md: admin@g2g.app/admin123, aria@g2g.app/demo1234, store@techhub.sg/demo1234, daniel@g2g.app/demo1234.
seed_data_creation: Test signups TEST_*@example.com + TEST_it3_* listings created via API for refund flow tests. Earlier polluted TEST_payment_* listings already present from iteration 2.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Public URL = https://g2g-marketplace.preview.emergentagent.com. Login: button text 'Sign in' (no type=submit). Aria's likes page now shows 2 items (TEST_payment_2027f4, TEST_dv_2daf15) from prior tests — preserves across reload, persisted via /api/likes/mine. Admin layout: NO Trust Score block, NO Chat / SmartMatch / AI-Valuation / Liked links in sidebar — matches spec. Leaflet imports work on /match. Wallet trust score sidebar for aria shows 49/100 'New Seller'. Outstanding: /api/admin/analytics is missing 3 fields and counts admin in users.
rca of the issue: N/A — no critical bugs. Two minor spec mismatches: (a) analytics fields missing — fix in server.py admin_analytics by adding open_reports = await db.reports.count_documents({status:{$nin:['resolved','dismissed','closed']}}), open_refunds similarly, verified_users = users where kycStatus=='approved' AND emailVerified, and filter users count {role:{$ne:'admin'}}; (b) new user trust score 10 vs 0 — change initial logic to require kycStatus=='approved' before email/phone bonuses or clamp to 0 until first verification step.
